title: Cyclosorus uniauriculatus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus uniauriculatus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other members of the family. Within the Thelypteridaceae family, Cyclosorus uniauriculatus is further categorized as a species within the genus Cyclosorus. The genus Cyclosorus is a part of the larger group known as the Thelypteridaceae family. The species Cyclosorus uniauriculatus is often classified as a type of vascular plant.
uses: Cyclosorus uniauriculatus has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant and antifungal properties, contributing to its medicinal value. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Cyclosorus uniauriculatus.

intro: Cyclosorus uniauriculatus, also known as the auriculated fern or button fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
